Title: Membrane anchoring facilitates colocalization of enzymes in plant cytochrome P450 redox systems

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NADPH-dependent cytochrome P450 oxidoreductase; diflavin oxidoreductase with FMN-binding domain, linker domain, FAD/NADPH-binding domain, and N-terminal transmembrane anchor
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports biosynthesis of diverse plant specialized metabolites by donating electrons to microsomal cytochrome P450 enzymes
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Membrane anchor of SbPOR2B promotes dynamic colocalization at membranes, implying enhanced organization of P450-dependent metabolic cascades rather than direct alteration of a single terminal metabolite
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A 36-aa transmembrane anchor peptide from Sorghum bicolor POR2B was reconstituted into biomimetic membranes and displayed two mobility modes. 2. Brightness tracking showed dynamic colocalization of POR anchors beyond random overlap. 3. Sequence comparison across land plants showed relative conservation of the POR transmembrane segment, supporting functional importance in organizing P450 redox systems.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Biomimetic lipid membranes/GUV system at physiologically relevant anchor density (~31 molecules per µm^2)
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Chemical synthesis of transmembrane anchor peptide; reconstitution in GUV membranes; single-molecule TIRF tracking; brightness colocalization analysis; stochastic simulations; comparative sequence conservation analysis
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative bioinformatic analysis of 278 land plant POR sequences from Phytozome v12, One Thousand Plant Transcriptomes, NCBI, and UniProt; no accession list provided in main text
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Membrane anchoring motifs from POR may be exploited to improve colocalization and coupling of heterologous enzymes in synthetic biology platforms for plant natural product production; caution that this paper does not test agronomic traits or metabolite yield directly
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study experimentally focuses on the isolated transmembrane anchor rather than full-length POR catalytic activity; POR is treated as a metabolic hub/electron donor common to many P450 pathways, not as a pathway enzyme for one specific nutrient metabolite.
